why_it_matters: An active injectable medication must be captured in the reconciliation to keep the medication list accurate and support ongoing anemia management.
Documented barriers to obtaining care directly affect the feasibility of the new medication, lab follow-up, and dental referral plans.
Prior migrant/seasonal farm work informs occupational exposure risk and social context but has limited immediate bearing on today's management.
These symptoms reflect the clinical consequence of missed B12 doses and justify continuing the injection, supporting adherence counseling.
Documenting the safety-net plan for worsening grief/depression ensures appropriate follow-up though current screening is low-risk.
